ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bread class; no specific pandan bread entry exists. Mapped to nearest parent class 'bread (food product)'.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a pandan-specific bread code. Mapped to the bread and similar products category A000E as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for pandan bread; general bread concept exists but pandan-flavoured variant is not separately coded.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of pandan bread is approximately 2 slices (60 g), commonly consumed at breakfast or as a snack in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Pandan Bread — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for pandan bread (F05030008). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: White bread GI reference — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to pandan bread
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for pandan bread exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Pandan bread is made from refined wheat flour with a small amount of pandan extract and sugar; its composition closely mirrors white sandwich bread (GI ~70–75, University of Sydney GI Database). A GI of 75 is applied as the analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (75 × 45.7 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 60 g serving) / 100 / 100 = 20.6 g GL per 60 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 319 mg per 100 g; borderline-high for a bread product, partially offset by dietary fibre 3.7 g per 100 g and potassium ~120 mg per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit pandan bread to 1–2 slices per serving, avoid pairing with high-sodium spreads such as salted butter or processed cheese, and account for cumulative sodium from other meal components to stay within the 2000 mg daily sodium target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI of 75 and carbohydrate load of 45.7 g per 100 g from refined wheat flour; dietary fibre at 3.7 g per 100 g provides partial mitigation but is insufficient to offset the rapid glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should restrict pandan bread to one slice per occasion, pair it with a protein source such as a boiled egg or low-fat cheese to blunt the postprandial glucose rise, avoid sugary spreads such as kaya, and prefer wholemeal pandan bread variants when available to lower the effective GI.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is low at approximately 1.2 g per 100 g and trans fat is negligible; however, sodium at 319 mg per 100 g and refined carbohydrate content are relevant cardiovascular risk factors at higher intake levels
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals managing cardiovascular disease, pandan bread is acceptable in moderate portions (1–2 slices) as part of a heart-healthy diet, provided it is not paired with high-saturated-fat or high-sodium spreads, and total daily sodium and refined carbohydrate intake are monitored.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.2 g per 100 g), meaningful dietary fibre (3.7 g per 100 g), and pandan extract containing chlorophyll and antioxidant compounds with mild anti-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pandan bread is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis when consumed in moderate portions; avoid adding butter or margarine with trans fat, as these would increase the pro-inflammatory saturated and trans fat load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pandan bread (班兰面包; Roti pandan) is a soft, lightly sweetened loaf bread popular in Singapore and across Southeast Asia, distinguished by its characteristic green colour and fragrant aroma derived from pandan (Pandanus amaryllifolius) extract or juice. The bread is made from refined wheat flour, yeast, sugar, salt, vegetable oil, milk powder, water, and pandan extract, then baked to produce a fluffy, pillowy crumb with a thin golden crust. Its flavour is mildly sweet and subtly grassy-floral, reminiscent of vanilla with a tropical herbal note. Pandan bread is commonly consumed at breakfast or as a snack, often eaten plain or with spreads such as kaya (coconut-egg jam), butter, or peanut butter. Per 100 g, it provides 266 kcal, 12.1 g protein, 3.9 g fat, 45.7 g carbohydrate, 3.7 g dietary fibre, and 319 mg sodium, with notably high calcium (336 mg) and iron (4.8 mg) likely reflecting fortification. The glycaemic index is high (~75), making portion control important for individuals with type 2 diabetes. The sodium content is borderline-elevated, warranting caution for those with hypertension. Pandan extract contributes chlorophyll and antioxidant compounds, lending mild anti-inflammatory properties. This food is suitable for vegetarians and is generally halal-certified in Singapore commercial bakeries.
common_user_queries: How many calories in pandan bread?
Is pandan bread high in sodium?
What is pandan bread made of?
Is pandan bread suitable for hypertension?
How much protein is in pandan bread?
What is the GI of pandan bread?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F05030008) at confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white bread as the reference food, which is the closest defensible approved-source equivalent. Saturated fat and potassium are estimated from standard bread composition data consistent with the HPB profile.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for pandan bread is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F05030008. cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: confirmed negligible per standard commercial bread formulation; no trans fat declared. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate based on white bread reference from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for pandan bread was found in any approved source. Calcium value of 336 mg per 100 g and iron of 4.8 mg per 100 g are unusually high for plain bread and likely reflect flour fortification as reported in the HPB source; these values are preserved as reported. Potassium of ~120 mg per 100 g is estimated from USDA SR Legacy white bread data as a supporting reference, consistent with the HPB profile.
